expectations.text: Response provides a TypeScript code block using server.registerTool or equivalent MCP registration
Tool description explains what the tool *returns* not just what it does
expectations.evidence: Description explicitly names return fields: 'Returns PR number, title, state, author login, labels, assignees, milestone, head/base branch names, draft status, and timestamps'

expectations.text: Input schema uses Zod with .describe() annotations on at least 2 fields
expectations.evidence: All 9 parameters use z.string/number/enum with .describe() — examples: owner, repo, state, head, base, sort, direction, per_page, page

expectations.text: Response includes readOnlyHint: true in the annotations
expectations.evidence: annotations: { readOnlyHint: true, destructiveHint: false, idempotentHint: true, openWorldHint: true }

expectations.text: Response includes pagination support (per_page or page parameter) with a max constraint
Response returns both content (text) and structuredContent in the tool handler
expectations.evidence: return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2) }], structuredContent: summary }
